news.summary: Rockwell Automation, Inc. (NYSE: ROK) will have its Chairman and CEO, Blake Moret, along with SVP and CFO, Christian Rothe, speaking at Wolfe’s 18th Annual Global Transportation & Industrials Conference on May 21, 2025, in New York. The webcast of their fireside chat is scheduled to start at around 9:10 a.m. EDT and can be accessed on the Rockwell Automation Investor Relations website at www.rockwellautomation.com/en-us/investors.html. Rockwell Automation is a well-known company in the industrial sector.
news.headline: Rockwell Automation (NYSE: ROK) to present at Wolfe’s 18th Annual Global Transportation & Industrials Conference on May 21, 2025 in New York. Fireside chat webcast at 9:10am EDT. Visit www.rockwellautomation.com/en-us/investors.html.

news.summary: In Denver, BKV Corporation (NYSE: BKV) disclosed its financial outcomes for Q1 2025, along with projections for Q2 2025. Key points from the first quarter include a partnership deal with Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners (CIP) for carbon capture endeavors. The company reported a net loss amounting to $78.7 million, or $(0.93) per diluted share.
news.headline: BKV Corporation Q1 2025 results: Net loss of $78.7 million, joint venture with CIP for CCUS projects.

news.summary: In Memphis, Tennessee, Sylvamo (NYSE: SLVM), the global paper company, has announced its first quarter 2025 earnings. In comparison to the fourth quarter, there was a significant decrease in financial performance. Net income for this quarter was $27 million, equivalent to $0.65 per diluted share, as opposed to the previous quarter's $81 million, or $1.94 per diluted share. Adjusted operating earnings were reported at $28 million ($0.68 per diluted share), down from $82 million ($1.96 per diluted share). Adjusted EBITDA stood at $90 million with an 11% margin, while the fourth quarter recorded $157 million with a 16% margin. The cash flow from operating activities was not explicitly stated.
news.headline: Sylvamo releases Q1 2025 earnings: Net income $27M, EPS $0.65 vs $81M, EPS $1.94. Adjusted EBITDA $90M vs $157M.

news.summary: In Toronto, Americas Gold and Silver Corporation (TSX: USA) (NYSE American: USAS) has released its consolidated financial and operational results for the quarter ending March 31, 2025. The company, a North American precious metals producer, reported on its earnings for the period. The detailed financial data, along with the Management’s Discussion and Analysis, Financial Statements, and Notes to Financial Statements for the same period, are now available on the company's website.
news.headline: Americas Gold and Silver (TSX: USA) reports Q1 2025 results in Toronto, including financial and operational details.

news.summary: Neo Performance Materials Inc. reported a strong Q1 2025 financial performance, showcasing a 59% increase in Adjusted EBITDA compared to the same period last year. The company's revenue for Q1 2025 was $121.6 million, with an operating income of $9.6 million. Adjusted Net Income for Q1 2025 was $3.6 million. Neo maintained a solid liquidity position with $77.3 million in cash and a net cash balance of $6.2 million. In terms of segment performance, Magnequench achieved an Adjusted EBITDA of $6.7 million, while Chemicals & Oxides reported $6.8 million in Adjusted EBITDA, and Rare Metals segment saw a slight decrease in Adjusted EBITDA to $8.6 million. Neo highlighted the progress on its major capital project of launching a European permanent magnet facility by 2026. Additionally, the company shipped its first sintered magnet samples to a Tier 1 motor customer from the European facility. Neo is also engineering a heavy rare earth pilot line at its Silmet facility. Furthermore, Neo completed the sale of majority equity interests in JAMR and ZAMR, generating $28.0 million in cash proceeds. The company signed a memorandum of understanding for the offtake of niobium pentoxide from the Kanyika Project in Malawi. Neo settled significant intellectual property litigation for €11.6 million in March 2025. The company continues its strategic review process and optimization of business operations. Overall, Neo is well-positioned for growth and emphasizing operational excellence and supply chain strengthening. The full news story is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Neo Performance Materials reports strong Q1 2025 results, including 59% increase in Adjusted EBITDA to $17.1 million and $121.6 million in revenue, indicating a positive trend for the company's financial performance and operational growth. Strategic investments in growth projects and supply chain operations are positioning Neo well for future success. Strategic business decisions such as divestitures and partnerships are enhancing the company's financial strength and operational efficiency. Key developments include achievements in Magnequench, Chemicals & Oxides, and Rare Metals segments, as well as progress in major capital projects focused on ramping up rare earth permanent magnet production capabilities in Europe. Furthermore, the company's solid liquidity position and ongoing strategic review process underpin its commitment to maximizing shareholder value and long-term growth prospects.

news.summary: Onex Corporation reported its financial results for the first quarter of 2025, with net earnings of $168 million, a significant increase from the $10 million reported in the same period in 2024. The net earnings per diluted share also saw a substantial jump to $2.36 from $0.13 in the previous year. Onex raised $2.5 billion in fee-generating capital in the first quarter. The company's investing segment net earnings were $123 million, with total segment net earnings reaching $148 million. Onex had approximately $8.3 billion in investing capital at the end of the quarter. The private equity investments generated net gains of $96 million, while the credit strategies investments yielded net gains of $11 million. The company also repurchased 1,379,506 Subordinate Voting Shares at a cost of $98 million. Onex's cash and near-cash balance stood at $1.6 billion as of March 31, 2025. The company declared a second quarter dividend of C$0.10 per Subordinate Voting Share. Onex had $36.9 billion of FGAUM at the end of the quarter, a 17% increase over the last 12 months. The management fees increased to $202 million as of March 31, 2025, and the company had unrealized carried interest totaling $308 million. Onex is listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange under the symbol ONEX and has approximately $53.1 billion in assets under management, with $8.3 billion being Onex's own investing capital. The company's financial statements are available on its website.
news.headline: Onex reports Q1 2025 results with net earnings of $168M, a $2.5B in capital raised since the start of the year, and $36.9B in FGAUM.

news.summary: Shortly after taking full control of its struggling Chinese chain, Restaurant Brands International Inc. (NYSE: QSR) is moving quickly to sell off its Burger King China operations. During its first quarter earnings call on Thursday, the fast food company announced that it was designating Burger King China as "held for sale" on its financial records and has enlisted Morgan Stanley Inc. to help find a new local majority shareholder. This decision follows the acquisition of the remaining equity in Burger King China from former partners in February. Despite facing challenges, Restaurant Brands has shown progress in reorganizing the business in the past three months. Mohamed El-Erian has raised concerns about the potential impact on domestic industries as China's exports grow by 8% while US shipments drop by 21%. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Restaurant Brands International is swiftly exiting Burger King China, citing complexity and financial strain. They are classifying the subsidiary as "held for sale" after acquiring full control in February. Morgan Stanley is assisting in finding a new local controlling shareholder.
French President Emmanuel Macron arrives at Place Stanislas ahead of defence treaty signing ceremony with Poland
Company Aclaris Therapeutics to join HC Wainwright's "HCW@Home" Series.

news.summary: On May 9, 2025, Rapid Micro Biosystems, Inc. (Nasdaq: RPID) reported its financial performance for the first quarter ending on March 31, 2025. The company, based in Lexington, Mass., specializes in life sciences technology and offers automation solutions to streamline healthcare product manufacturing and ensure their quick and secure distribution. Rapid Micro Biosystems disclosed its earnings for this period, showcasing its commitment to advancing automation in the life sciences sector.

news.summary: Kymera's IRF5 program bolsters their oral immunology lineup, offering a supplementary approach to broaden their reach to rheumatic and various autoimmune conditions. This initiative aims to develop a top-tier oral medication in this field.

news.summary: Osteotec, based in Newbury, England, has recently revealed a significant collaboration with icotec, a global frontrunner in non-metallic spinal implants tailored for spine tumor treatment. The agreement establishes Osteotec as the sole distributor of icotec's innovative medical devices. This partnership brings together two prominent companies to advance the availability of specialized healthcare solutions.
news.headline: Osteotec & icotec ag sign exclusive agreement to advance spine tumour therapy in Great Britain on May 9, 2025.

news.summary: Enbridge Inc. shared its financial results for the first quarter of 2025 in Calgary, AB, under the tickers TSX: ENB and NYSE: ENB. The company reaffirmed its financial guidance for 2025 and offered a quarterly business update. The figures presented are unaudited.
news.headline: Enbridge Inc. reports record quarterly results, reaffirms 2025 financial guidance, illustrating strong business model.

news.summary: The app has experienced significant growth, with its user base expanding to almost 300,000 users. It has shown strong performance in both Lifetime Value (LTV) and Return on Investment (ROI). Moreover, there has been a rapid increase in user-generated content on social media platforms. This information comes from a recent report about Siyata Mobile Inc., a global company based in Vancouver, BC. The report was shared on May 9, 2025, by PRNewswire. Siyata Mobile Inc. is listed on Nasdaq under the symbols SYTA and SYTAW.

news.summary: In the latest financial report released by Construction Partners, Inc. (NASDAQ: ROAD) based in Dothan, Alabama, it was revealed that the company experienced a significant increase in revenue, up by 54% compared to Q2 of FY24. The net income for this quarter stood at $4.2 million, with earnings per share (EPS) at $0.08. Adjusted EBITDA also saw a substantial rise, increasing by 135% compared to the same quarter in the previous fiscal year. Additionally, Construction Partners reported a record backlog of projects amounting to $2.84 billion. As a result of these positive results, the company has raised its outlook for the fiscal year 2025.
news.headline: Construction Partners, Inc. reports Q2 FY25 revenue up 54%, net income $4.2 million, EPS $0.08, adjusted EBITDA up 135%. Record backlog of $2.84 billion.

news.summary: In the most recent quarter, the company reported quarterly GAAP earnings of $0.33 per diluted share and Distributable Earnings (DE) of $0.45 per diluted share. They made investments totaling $2.3 billion during the quarter, with $1.4 billion allocated to commercial lending and a record-breaking $0.7 billion in infrastructure lending. Additionally, they finalized $1.3 billion in investments shortly after the quarter ended, including $0.9 billion in unspecified transactions.
news.headline: Starwood Property Trust Q1 2025: GAAP Earnings $0.33 per share, DE $0.45 per share. Invested $2.3B, $1.4B in commercial lending. Closed $1.3B post-quarter.

news.summary: Celebrating ambitious entrepreneurs shaping the future, Ernst & Young LLP (EY US) revealed the finalists for the prestigious Entrepreneur Of The Year 2025 Mid-Atlantic Award. This year marks the event's 40th anniversary. The announcement was made in Fairfax, Va., on May 9, 2025. The award recognizes outstanding entrepreneurs who demonstrate innovative thinking and leadership. This year's finalists showcase exemplary talent and vision within the industry. This event highlights the achievements of individuals driving progress and growth in the region.
news.headline: Gloria Bohan of Omega World Travel named EY Entrepreneur Of The Year® 2025, shaping future for ambitious entrepreneurs.

news.summary: On May 9, 2025, MediPharm Labs Corp. (TSX: LABS) (OTCQB: MEDIF) (FSE: MLZ), a pharmaceutical company focusing on precision-based cannabinoids, has revealed its intention to unveil its financial results for the first quarter.
news.headline: MediPharm Labs (TSX: LABS) to report Q1 2025 financial results; announcement on May 9, 2025.

news.summary: In the initial three months of 2025, there was a net loss of $162.0 million, equivalent to a basic loss of $1.87 per common share. The Adjusted EBITDA for the same period was $55.0 million, which includes a $30.4 million amendment for RINs incurrence expenses and $16.9 million from the Production Tax Credit.
news.headline: Calumet reports first quarter 2025 net loss of $162.0M, adjusted EBITDA $55.0M. Adjustments include $30.4M RINs expense and $16.9M from PTC.

news.summary: The Federal Agricultural Mortgage Corporation (Farmer Mac) reported an outstanding business volume of $29.8 billion. Farmer Mac, listed on NYSE as AGM and AGM.A, is a key secondary market provider in the nation. Its primary goal is to enhance accessibility to financing, ensuring vital liquidity for various sectors. This information was disclosed on May 9, 2025, in Washington.

news.summary: New findings regarding the safety and effectiveness of iza-bren in Locally Advanced or Metastatic Breast Cancer will be shared at a conference. These results stem from a Phase 1 clinical trial evaluating iza-bren, an EGFRxHER3 bispecific topoisomerase inhibitor-based antibody-drug conjugate. The presentation will take place in Redmond, Washington, on May 9, 2025.
news.headline: SystImmune presenting updated data on iza-bren in breast cancer at ESMO 2025, a Ph1 study for locally advanced or metastatic cases.

news.summary: 1stdibs.com, Inc. (NASDAQ: DIBS), a prominent online luxury design product marketplace, has released its financial results for the first quarter ending on March 31, 2025. In this quarter, the company saw a 2% year-over-year rise in net revenue, amounting to $22.5 million. Gross profit also increased by 2% compared to the previous year, reaching $16.3 million. The gross margin for this quarter was reported at 72.4%, slightly lower than the 72.5% recorded in the first quarter of 2024. Additionally, they disclosed a GAAP net loss of $4.8 million for the quarter.
news.headline: 1stdibs reported Q1 2025 financial results; net revenue $22.5M, gross profit $16.3M, GAAP net loss $4.8M.

news.summary: On May 9, 2025, Tiziana Life Sciences, Ltd. (Nasdaq: TLSA) disclosed findings from a PET scan demonstrating a notable decline in microglia activation linked to neuroinflammation in a patient with moderate Alzheimer's disease (AD). The patient had undergone a three-month treatment with intranasal foralumab, a fully human anti-CD3 monoclonal antibody, through an expanded access program. The microglial TSPO PET scan exhibited a substantial reduction in microglia activation, a crucial marker of neuroinflammation connected to the advancement of Alzheimer's disease.
news.headline: Tiziana Life Sciences reports significant reduction in microglia activation in moderate Alzheimer's patient after three months of intranasal foralumab, shown by PET scan.

news.summary: A recent report reveals that the total number of COVID-19 cases in the country has surged to over 1.5 million, with an alarming increase of 10,000 new cases in the past 24 hours. The death toll has also risen to 25,000, marking a significant rise in fatalities. Health officials are urging the public to adhere to safety guidelines, including wearing masks and practicing social distancing, to curb the spread of the virus. Additionally, vaccination efforts continue to ramp up, with over 500,000 doses administered nationwide. The government is working diligently to ensure vaccine accessibility for all eligible individuals.

news.summary: A report on the Uzbekistan Construction Market by ResearchAndMarkets.com projects a 5.6% real terms expansion in the industry in 2025. The report covers sectors such as Commercial, Industrial, Infrastructure, Energy and Utilities, Institutional, and Residential markets, providing analysis up to the first half of 2029. This growth is attributed to investments in transportation and renewable energy projects, with a focus on public and private funding to drive development. The construction market in Uzbekistan shows promising trends and opportunities for various sectors, indicating positive economic prospects for the coming years.
news.headline: Uzbekistan construction market to grow at 5.2% annually by 2029, estimated 5.6% real term expansion in 2025.

news.summary: In Delray Beach, Florida on May 9, 2025, a report was released regarding the Fatty Amines Market. The analysis also includes factors such as function, carbon chain length, and regional distribution globally. This comprehensive study provides insights into this market segment for businesses and investors.
news.headline: Fatty Amines Market projected to reach $4.97 billion by 2030 per MarketsandMarkets™ report.

The EIT FAN cohort of 2025 marks a significant milestone in accelerating the growth of promising food-related ventures.

news.summary: TELUS Digital Experience, a prominent global technology company focused on digital customer experiences, has recently disclosed its financial performance for the quarter ending on March 31, 2025. TELUS Corporation, serving as the main shareholder of TELUS Digital, also shared the results. The figures presented, both in this report and other TELUS Digital publications, are denoted in U.S. dollars unless indicated otherwise.
